meta.desc: For CertificateEntry in a server Certificate message, extensions MUST correspond to extensions offered in the ClientHello message.

relevant_snippets.reason: Strongly related. Defines the core TLS extension list structure and declares APIs specifically for TLS 1.3 Certificate message extensions, which is central to tracking whether CertificateEntry extensions correspond to those offered in ClientHello.

Strongly related by pattern. Shows explicit validation that a received extension in a server response must have been requested by the client; this is analogous to the target rule for CertificateEntry extensions corresponding to ClientHello-offered extensions.

Weakly related. Defines one concrete extension type and helper APIs to retrieve extension requests from the ClientHello-side extension list. Useful context for how offered/requested extensions are represented and queried.

Weakly related. Another concrete extension definition with APIs to get the requested extension data and to enable/use the extension, giving context on how ClientHello extension offers are stored and later consulted.

Weakly related. Certificate status request v2 is a certificate-related extension and includes extension list accessors. It may be one of the extension types relevant to CertificateEntry processing, but this snippet does not show the actual correspondence validation.

result.summary: Most candidates are unrelated. The key retained context is the TLSX extension-list definition in internal.h, because the target variable 'extensions' in TLS 1.3 CertificateEntry processing is almost certainly represented through this structure. A strong behavioral analogue is the DTLS Connection ID parser, which rejects a server-side extension that was not requested by the client, matching the business rule pattern. SNI/ALPN/CSRv2 snippets are weaker but useful examples of concrete extension representations and request retrieval APIs.
